ucs.title: [LLM] TA551 (Shathak/GOLD CABIN) password-protected ZIP → Word macro → regsvr32 DLL loader chain
ucs.description: Hunts TA551's signature delivery-to-install chain: a thread-hijacked email with a password-protected ZIP attachment yields a Word doc whose macro drops a randomly-named DLL/HTA into %ProgramData% or %AppData% and executes it via regsvr32.exe or mshta.exe. This loader pattern delivers IcedID, Valak, Ursnif, and Qakbot — all payloads tied directly to TA551 by Unit 42, Secureworks (GOLD CABIN), and Proofpoint.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","wmiprvse.exe")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe")) AND (Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*") AND (Processes.process="*.dll*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*" OR Processes.process="*.ocx*" OR Processes.process="*.txt*") by host Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdline "(?i)(?<payload_path>[A-Za-z]:\\\\[^\"]+\.(dll|hta|ocx|txt))" | rex field=payload_path "\\\\(?<payload_file>[^\\\\]+)$" | eval suspicious_name=if(match(payload_file,"(?i)^[a-z0-9]{4,12}\.(dll|hta|ocx|txt)$"),1,0) | where suspicious_name=1 OR match(parent_process_name,"(?i)winword|excel") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","wmiprvse.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let lolbins = dynamic(["regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
let suspectPaths = dynamic(["\\ProgramData\\","\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\","\\AppData\\Roaming\\","\\Users\\Public\\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
| where FileName in~ (lolbins)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (suspectPaths)
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.(dll|hta|ocx|txt|jpg)(\b|""|$)"
| extend payload = extract(@"(?i)([A-Za-z]:\\[^"" ]+\.(?:dll|hta|ocx|txt|jpg))", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend payloadName = tostring(split(payload, "\\")[-1])
| extend randomLooking = iff(payloadName matches regex @"(?i)^[a-z0-9]{4,14}\.(dll|hta|ocx|txt|jpg)$", true, false)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
    | project DeviceId, DropTime=Timestamp, DroppedFile=FolderPath, DroppedName=FileName, DropProc=InitiatingProcessFileName
) on DeviceId
| where isempty(DroppedFile) or DroppedFile has_any (suspectPaths)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, payload, payloadName, randomLooking, DropTime, DroppedFile, DroppedName, ReportId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: TA551's loader is unusually rigid: Word/Excel macro spawns regsvr32 (or occasionally mshta/rundll32) against a short, randomly-named DLL/HTA written to ProgramData, AppData\Roaming, or %TEMP%. Unit 42 (Jan 2021) and Proofpoint repeatedly fingerprinted this exact parent→child→path→short-name combination across IcedID/Valak/Ursnif/Qakbot deliveries. Filtering on all four conditions simultaneously (O

ucs.title: [LLM] TA551 thread-hijacked phish with password-protected ZIP attachment delivering macro doc
ucs.description: Detects TA551's hallmark delivery vector: reply-chain (thread-hijacked) emails to English/German/Italian/Japanese recipients carrying a small password-protected ZIP whose password appears in the email body, containing a maldoc that subsequently triggers the regsvr32 loader chain. Correlating EmailEvents with the on-host UrlClick/Attachment open closes the loop.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Email.recipient) as recipient values(All_Email.src_user) as sender values(All_Email.subject) as subject values(All_Email.file_name) as attachment values(All_Email.message_id) as msg_id from datamodel=Email where (All_Email.file_name="*.zip" OR All_Email.file_name="*.iso" OR All_Email.file_name="*.img") by host All_Email.recipient All_Email.src_user | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Email)` | eval thread_hijack=if(match(subject,"(?i)^(re:|fwd:|aw:|tr:|r:|antw:)"),1,0) | eval pw_hint=if(match(subject,"(?i)password|kennwort|contraseña|パスワード|mot de passe|parola") OR match(subject,"(?i)invoice|rechnung|fattura|請求|facture"),1,0) | where thread_hijack=1 AND pw_hint=1 | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe") by host Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.rationale: Thread-hijack subject prefix + small password-protected archive + multilingual lure language (DE/IT/JA/EN — exactly the targeting Secureworks attributes to GOLD CABIN/Shathak) is a TA551-specific delivery fingerprint rare in commodity phish. Joining the email arrival to the same recipient's host firing a regsvr32-from-Office-with-AppData-DLL within four hours requires both halves of TA551's chain
